reasoning: The document shows catastrophic content loss and structural failure, with shingling recall (0.636) confirming the LLM's report that approximately 50% of the text is missing, specifically in sections 6 and 8. The structural score (0.545) and broken heading hierarchy further indicate that the document's organization was not preserved during conversion.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: There is a massive omission of body text in sections 6 and 8. Specifically, the conversion includes the names of the committees and sub-section headings but excludes all the corresponding paragraphs describing the status reports and other items. In Section 6, the text for parts a, b, and c (on page 2) and parts d and e (on page 3) is entirely missing. In Section 8, the body text for parts a and b (on page 3) is entirely missing. This represents roughly 50% of the document's total content.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 2, Section 6.a: 'The LGC last met on November 20th...Debbie thanked Anna Verrill for her hard work... Workshops will be scheduled...' Page 3, Section 8.a: 'Hope reported that Esri has offered to host dynamic web services...'
concerns.output_quote: The JSON output for Section 6 (reading orders 24 and 26) contains only the committee names in list form. The JSON output for Section 8 (reading order 30) contains only the titles 'a. LiDAR Data' and 'b. Future M&O Meetings' without the descriptive body text.

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is inconsistent and fails to reflect the source document's organization. The committee name is correctly set as H1 and 'MINUTES' as H2. However, the document's main sections are numbered 1 through 9. While sections 6, 7, 8, and 9 are correctly assigned H2 headings, sections 1 through 5 are incorrectly rendered as list items within five separate single-item lists. This creates a disjointed hierarchy where headings of equal weight in the source are treated differently in the

2. Approval of Minutes...
3. NC Parcels Update...
4. Census Update...

concerns.analysis: The list structure is fundamentally flawed in several ways. First, the main numbered items (1-5) are split into five separate unordered lists, and their prefix numbers are lost. Second, the sub-items for Section 6 (a, b, c, d, e) and Section 8 (a, b) are identified as list items, but the parser failed to include the body paragraphs that immediately follow each item in the source, resulting in massive text omission. concerns.output_quote: List elements at reading_order 24, 26, and 30 contain only the item headers, while the body text associated with those list items is entirely missing from the output.

However, in the HTML output, these sections are rendered as simple bulleted or plain-text lists of the subheadings only. action_items: Restore all missing body text paragraphs in Sections 6 (Status Reports) and 8 (Other Items).
Correct the heading structure for items 1-5 to match the weight of items 6-9.
Re-verify the list numbering (1-5 and a-e) which was lost or inconsistent during conversion.
